# Sponsor site snapshot — fernway-parcel.example

Text captured the way a builder actually pastes it on event day: headings
and copy, no assets. All URLs are fictional and unresolvable by design.

---

## https://fernway-parcel.example/ (homepage)

**Fernway Parcel — every package, accounted for.**

Fernway moves parcels for small shops that promise real delivery windows.
Our scan network posts a parcel's journey within seconds of each touch —
pickup, line-haul, last mile — and every scan carries a checksum your
systems can verify independently. No dashboards to buy, no black boxes:
one API, honest events.

- **Fernway Trace** — live parcel status for your storefront or app.
- **Fernway Notify** — webhook pushes when a parcel changes state.
  *(Marked "early access" on the site; no public documentation.)*
- Developers → https://fernway-parcel.example/developers

## https://fernway-parcel.example/trace (product page)

**Fernway Trace**

Give recipients the answer to the only question they have: *where is it
now?* Trace returns the latest scan event for any parcel ID, with location,
timestamp, and a verifiable checksum — so what you show a customer is
provably what our network recorded, not a cached guess.

Plans: **Counter** (per-lookup), **Storefront** (monthly), **Freight**
(volume). Hackathon teams: ask at the booth for temporary credentials to
the sandbox environment.

> "We put Trace on our order page in an afternoon. Support tickets asking
> 'where's my package?' fell by half." — a claimed customer quote, as seen
> on the site; treat marketing numbers as tone, not data.
